opendb_id.description: Unique identifier for the document (UUID v4)

color.description: The color(s) of this product. Each color of the product should be included in the array. The color of the fan frame and blades.

quantity.description: Number of separately mountable fan units included in the package. Use the total number of fans only for multi-packs of separate fans. Single-frame multi-fan products count as 1 unit. Example: an NZXT F360-style 360mm single-frame triple-fan unit should use quantity 1, not 3.

min_airflow.description: The minimum airflow of the fan in CFM. If it is a constant, just fill in min_airflow and ignore max_airflow

max_airflow.description: The maximum airflow of the fan in CFM. If it is a constant, just fill in min_airflow and ignore max_airflow

min_noise_level.description: The minimum noise level of the fan in dB. If it is a constant, just fill in min_noise_level and ignore max_noise_level

max_noise_level.description: The maximum noise level of the fan in dB. If it is a constant, just fill in min_noise_level and ignore max_noise_level

pwm.description: Whether the fan supports Pulse Width Modulation for speed control

led.description: The type of LED lighting, if any (e.g., 'Addressable RGB', 'RGB', 'None')

connector.description: The type of connector(s) the fan uses (e.g., '4-pin PWM + 3-pin 5V Addressable RGB')

controller.description: If the fan includes a controller, specify the type (e.g., '5V Addressable RGB'). Otherwise, indicate 'None'

static_pressure.description: The static pressure of the fan in mmH₂O

isOEM.description: Whether this fan is OEM-only (bundled with cases, not sold separately)

general_product_information.description: General product information and retailer SKUs

manufacturer.description: The company that produced the case fans.

series.description: Product series name The manufacturer's marketed product line / family for the fan. Manufacturer is stored separately; `size`, `quantity` (pack count), `manufacturer_color`, `pwm`, `led`, and `connector` are also separate fields and MUST NOT be in the series. The series name often encodes the size (e.g., `NF-A12x25` is a 120mm family) because the manufacturer markets the same family with different SKUs per size — keep the size in the series name when the manufacturer's SKU does. Examples: 'Noctua NF-A12x25 PWM 120mm' → series='NF-A12x25'. 'Lian Li UNI Fan SL120 V2 3-Pack' → series='UNI Fan SL'. 'Corsair iCUE LINK QX120 RGB Triple Pack' → series='iCUE LINK QX120'. The series stays consistent across pack-size / color / PWM SKUs of the same product line.

variant.description: Specific variant in the series Most fans should have an empty variant. Pack quantity, fan size, color, PWM, LED, connector, and flow direction are all stored in their own fields. Only fill `variant` when the manufacturer ships a genuinely distinct sub-model under the same series name that is NOT already captured by another field. Examples: 'Arctic P12 PWM PST 5-Pack' → variant='PST' (PST = PWM Sharing Technology, not captured by `pwm`). 'Lian Li UNI Fan SL120 V2 3-Pack' → variant='V2' (a marketed hardware revision sold next to the V1 SKU). 'Corsair iCUE LINK QX120 RGB Starter Kit' → variant='Starter Kit' (the bundle SKU is sold separately). The variant must be unique within a (manufacturer, series, size, manufacturer_color, quantity) group; if every fan collapses to the same value, leave it empty.

manufacturer_color.description: Manufacturer's official color name for this product variant. The manufacturer's marketed colorway / frame finish name, verbatim. Common values: 'Black', 'White', 'Black / White'. Multi-tone fans use ' / '. Branded special / collab colorways belong here when the rest of the SKU is identical (e.g., 'Hatsune Miku Edition'). Noctua's signature colorways ('Brown / Beige', 'chromax.black', 'chromax.black.swap') also go here. Most stock fans are 'Black'. Do NOT put fan size, pack count, PWM, PST, RGB, ARGB, or family / model words like 'NF-A12x25', 'P12', 'UNI Fan' in manufacturer_color — those belong in dedicated fields or the series.

last_manually_spec_verified_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the part specs were last manually verified by a human
